title: No-circular-authority checker graph
purpose: Represent the declared authority dependencies among public self-verification checkers and reject any cycle in which a checker conclusion can re-enter its own premise chain.

nodes.conclusion: public package entry and script surface matches the current public-review baseline
minimal proof kernel coordinate and final-emission quarantine accept
explicit trust base is represented, non-empty, checksum-bound, and non-activating
trust-base shrink plan is represented, dependency-tracked, and non-activating
rule-family coverage seed ledger is represented and non-activating
seed checker-totality fuzz surface returns accept or reject without throwing
negative checker mutation seed suite rejects every configured mutation
static checker dependency graph artifacts are generated from the current checkout
declared authority graph is acyclic and no checker conclusion re-enters its own premise chain
one-command verifier accepted the integrated public-review self-verification stack

authorityEdges.description: The one-command verifier includes the public package surface as a guarded public entrypoint condition through release audit replay.
The rule-family coverage ledger uses the minimal kernel as evidence for final-prefix and rule-spine coverage.
The shrink-plan checker verifies that the plan targets the current explicit trust-base coordinate.
The rule-family coverage ledger uses the explicit trust-base ledger as evidence for TrustBase and complexity-family boundaries.
The rule-family coverage ledger uses the shrink plan as evidence for represented future coverage obligations.
The totality audit fuzzes CheckMinimalKernel0 and records its accept/reject behavior.
The totality audit fuzzes CheckTrustBase0 and records its accept/reject behavior.
The totality audit fuzzes CheckTrustBaseShrinkPlan0 and records its accept/reject behavior.
The totality audit fuzzes CheckPublicEntryReleaseSurface0 and records its accept/reject behavior.
The negative mutation suite mutates minimal-kernel proof and boundary fields and requires reject.
The negative mutation suite mutates trust-base kind, boundary, assumptions, checksum, and non-empty status.
The negative mutation suite mutates shrink-plan kind, boundary, dependencies, and completion status.
The negative mutation suite mutates public package surface drift and requires reject.
The cycle audit consumes a freshly generated static checker dependency graph as context.
The cycle audit verifies that the declared public-surface authority node does not re-enter itself through the authority graph.
The cycle audit verifies that the declared minimal-kernel authority node does not re-enter itself through the authority graph.
The cycle audit verifies that the declared trust-base authority node does not re-enter itself through the authority graph.
The cycle audit verifies that the declared shrink-plan authority node does not re-enter itself through the authority graph.
The cycle audit verifies that the rule-family coverage node does not re-enter itself through the authority graph.
The one-command verifier includes the checker-totality seed audit.
The one-command verifier includes the negative checker mutation seed audit.
The one-command verifier includes the rule-family coverage ledger audit.
The one-command verifier includes checker dependency graph generation.
The one-command verifier includes the no-circular-authority audit.
